preamble: The shift from the voiceless alveolar stop [t] to the voiced labio-velar approximant [w] is a complex sound change involving both backing and lenition. This rare transformation may proceed through several intermediate stages, including labialization of the alveolar stop.

examples.note: In specific historical contexts, the original stop lenited to a glide.

sources: Lynch, J. (1998). Pacific Languages: An Introduction.
